About: It is about the Bleeding Kansas period of 1854–1860.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Kansas State Capitol building.
It is associated with the Bleeding Kansas period.
It is associated with the Civil War.
It is associated with John Brown.
It is associated with William Allen White.
It is associated with the Kansas Murals Commission.
It is associated with Governor Walter Huxman.
It is associated with Thomas Hart Benton.
It is associated with the Agricultural College of the University of Wisconsin.
It is associated with the Spencer Museum.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the abolitionist John Brown with a Bible and a rifle.
It consists of Union and Confederate soldiers, living and dead.
It consists of a tornado and a prairie fire.
It consists of emigrants with covered wagons.
It consists of Franciscan missionary Fray Juan de Padilla.
It consists of a plainsman who has just killed a buffalo.
CreatedBy: It was created by the American artist John Steuart Curry.
CreatedFor: It was created for the Kansas State Capitol building.
HasA: It has a depiction of John Brown.
It has a depiction of a tornado.
It has a depiction of a prairie fire.
It has a depiction of emigrants with covered wagons.
It has a depiction of Union and Confederate soldiers.
It has a depiction of Fray Juan de Padilla.
It has a depiction of Coronado.
It has a depiction of a plainsman.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the fratricidal fury that first flamed on the plains of Kansas.
It is characterized by the destruction of the coming Civil War.
It is characterized by the wild zeal of the extremist.
It is characterized by the prototype of a great many Kansans.
HeldIn: It was held in the Capitol.
IsA: It is a study for a mural.
KnownFor: It is known for being John Steuart Curry's most famous work.
It is known for being the only work of Curry's to have a book devoted solely to it.
It is known for being the source material for the album cover of rock band Kansas' 1974 debut record.
It is known for its depiction of John Brown.
It is known for its depiction of the Bleeding Kansas period.
LocatedIn: It is located on the east side of the second floor rotunda of the Kansas State Capitol building.
It is located in Topeka, Kansas.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Kansas State Capitol building.
PartOf: It is part of the Kansas State Capitol building.
It is part of the Kansas Murals Commission project.
It is part of John Steuart Curry's Capitol project.
PublishedAs: It was published as the album cover of rock band Kansas' 1974 debut record.
UsedAs: It is used as album cover art.
It is used as the source material for album covers.
UsedFor: It is used for poster recreations.
It is used for depicting the "tragic prelude" to the Civil War.
It is used for depicting John Brown.
It is used for depicting the Bleeding Kansas period.
It is used for depicting the history of Kansas.
UsedIn: It is used in the Kansas State Capitol building.
It is used in the second floor rotunda.
UsedTo: It is used to express the fratricidal fury that first flamed on the plains of Kansas.
It is used to express the tragic prelude to the last bloody feud of the English-speaking people.
It is used to symbolize the destruction of the coming Civil War.
